title: Paspalum ternatum
taxonomy: Paspalum ternatum belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This family includes various types of grasses and other monocotyledonous plants. Paspalum ternatum is further classified into the genus Paspalum, which comprises around 250 species of grasses. The genus Paspalum is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Within the genus Paspalum, Paspalum ternatum is classified as a species of grass that is native to South America.
uses: Paspalum ternatum, also known as Paspalidium, has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world.
